title: qPCR for Authentication of Imported Meat and Dairy Products
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Global   food     trade     continues     to     expand  ,   increasing     the     risk     of     fraudulent     or     mislabeled     animal-derived     products     entering     domestic     markets  .   Authentication     of     imported     meat     and     dairy     products     is     essential     to     ensure     food     safety  ,   protect     consumers  ,   and     uphold     trade     integrity  .   Quantitative     Polymerase     Chain     Reaction   (  qPCR  )   provides     a     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     highly     specific     method     to     identify     animal     species   in   food     products  ,   even   in   processed     or     mixed     samples  .   Its     use     allows     regulatory     authorities     to     detect     adulteration  ,   prevent     food     fraud  ,   and     ensure     compliance     with     import     regulations  .   Incorporating     qPCR   testing   into   border   inspection     protocols     strengthens     traceability     and     consumer     confidence     while     reducing     the     risk     of     economic     losses     due     to     recalls     and     trade     disputes  .
